_notes.design: Each evaluator is shown run 1 baseline responses from their paired evaluatee for all I1 and I2 questions. Pairs are selected on the basis of institutional and regulatory contrast, so that cross-model normative divergence is maximized and asymmetric self-positioning is most likely to be detectable.
_notes.asymmetry_check: Compare each evaluator's per-dimension peer ratings against their own I3 strictness profile (inverted mean score per dimension across all scenarios). A model that holds peers to stricter standards than it revealed in I3 exhibits outward asymmetry. A model that is more lenient with peers than its own I3 profile suggests exhibits inward asymmetry.
_notes.elp_hook: Once I1 qualitative coding is complete, add 'institutional_reference' and 'democratic_preconditions' coding per model to each entry under 'evaluator_i1_coding' and 'evaluatee_i1_coding'. These will be used to compute the qualitative axis of the Epistemic Legitimacy Profile.

pairs.rationale: U.S. safety-focused (Anthropic) vs. China state-adjacent (DeepSeek). Maximum institutional contrast on regulatory philosophy and state-market relationship. Tests whether a model embedded in a rights/safety-centered context evaluates governance reasoning from a state-adjacent context asymmetrically.

Reciprocal of P1. China state-adjacent evaluating U.S. safety-focused. Both directions are required to detect directional asymmetry — if DeepSeek rates Claude differently than Claude rates DeepSeek on structurally equivalent reasoning, the asymmetry is institutionally patterned.

U.S. commercial (OpenAI) vs. EU-proximate (Mistral). Tests whether differing regulatory environments — U.S. market-led vs. EU rights/precautionary — produce asymmetric normative self-positioning on accountability and enforceability in particular, where EU and U.S. frameworks diverge most sharply.

Reciprocal of P3. EU-proximate evaluating U.S. commercial. If Mistral's legal certainty and enforceability framings are more rights-centered and state-centered than GPT-4o's, it should rate GPT-4o's market-centered framings lower — and vice versa in P3.

U.S. platform/public sector (Google) vs. China state-adjacent (DeepSeek). Gemini's extensive public sector deployment history creates a distinct accountability and enforceability framing compared to DeepSeek. Tests whether platform-oriented governance reasoning evaluates state-adjacent reasoning asymmetrically.
